challenges.prompt: useContext returns the default 'light', ignoring the value the app set. Why?
options: There's no <ThemeContext.Provider> above Btn, so it falls back to the createContext default
useContext only works in class components
challenges.explanation: Without a <ThemeContext.Provider value={...}> ancestor, consumers receive the default passed to createContext. Wrap the subtree in a Provider to broadcast a real value.

challenges.prompt: Order the three steps to use Context.
lines: Read it in any descendant: const theme = useContext(ThemeContext)
challenges.explanation: Create the context, provide a value to a subtree with the Provider, then consume it anywhere below with useContext — no prop-drilling.

challenges.prompt: What does Context primarily let you avoid?
challenges.code: // theme needed by a deeply nested button
options: Prop-drilling — threading the value through every intermediate component
challenges.explanation: Context distributes a value to any descendant directly, so you don't have to pass it as props through components that don't use it.
